title: MLB blocks dugout apps that recommend AI-assisted game decisions
friendlyTitle: Baseball limits AI advice during live games
summary: Major League Baseball has disabled custom dugout iPad tabs that teams used for recommendations on substitutions, pitch calling and other in-game choices. League-provided video and data remain available, but real-time strategic recommendations now sit outside the tablets’ permitted purpose.
friendlySummary: Major League Baseball has stopped teams from using custom dugout iPad apps that recommend substitutions, pitches and other game decisions. Teams can still view approved video and data.
whyItMatters: The rule draws a practical line between information access and automated decision support in a competitive setting. Workplaces, schools and professional bodies will increasingly need similarly specific policies that define which decisions AI may inform and which must remain human.
friendlyWhyItMatters: Rules work better when they clearly say what AI may and may not do. Other organisations can use the same approach for important decisions.
